label: Person Identifier
description: Unique identifier for each individual in the panel
notes: Cross-sectional unit. Use as panel identifier (xtset person_id year).

label: Survey Year
description: Calendar year of the observation
notes: Time identifier. Use with person_id to declare panel (xtset person_id year).

label: Hourly Wage (USD)
description: Gross hourly wage in current-year US dollars. Missing (.) when unemployed.
notes: Set to 0.00 or missing (.) when employed=0. Always use ln_wage in regressions to reduce skew.

label: Log Hourly Wage
description: Natural logarithm of wage_hourly. Missing (.) when unemployed.
notes: Missing coded as Stata system missing (.). Standard dependent variable for Mincer earnings equations.

label: Age in Years
description: Respondent's age at survey date
notes: Increases by 1 each year per person by design.

label: Years of Schooling
description: Completed years of formal education at time of survey
notes: Values: 10=less than HS, 11=some HS, 12=HS diploma, 13=some college, 14=Associate/vocational, 16=Bachelor, 18=Master, 20=Doctorate/professional

label: Potential Labour Market Experience
description: Years since leaving full-time education (age - education_years - 6)
notes: Often called potential experience (Mincer, 1974). Always include experience_sq alongside this variable.

label: Experience Squared
description: Square of experience. Captures concavity of age-earnings profile.
notes: Defined as experience^2. Include to model diminishing returns.

label: Gender
description: Respondent's gender
notes: Binary in this dataset. Use i.gender in Stata factor notation.

label: Race/Ethnicity
description: Respondent's self-identified race or ethnicity
notes: Use i.race in factor notation. Omit one category as reference.

label: US Census Region
description: Geographic region of residence
notes: Use i.region in factor notation.

label: Industry Sector
description: Broad industry classification of employment
values_or_range: Manufacturing / Retail / Finance / Healthcare / Hospitality / Construction / Education / Technology
notes: Coded from SIC/NAICS. Use i.industry in factor notation.

label: Occupation Group
description: Occupational classification
values_or_range: Manager / Professional / Analyst / Operative / Craft Worker / Sales Worker / Service Worker
notes: Use i.occupation in factor notation.

label: Employment Status
description: Whether the respondent was employed during the survey reference week
notes: wage_hourly and ln_wage are missing when employed=0.

label: Union Membership
description: Whether the respondent is a current union or collective agreement member
notes: Key variable for union wage premium estimation.

label: Employer Firm Size
description: Size category of the employing firm
notes: Proxy for ability-to-pay and wage-setting power.

label: Part-Time Status
description: Whether the respondent works fewer than 35 hours per week
notes: Correlated with hours_week and wage_hourly.

label: Job Tenure
description: Years with current employer. Zero when not employed.
notes: Reset to 0 on job loss or change. Use to estimate returns to firm-specific capital.

label: Usual Hours Worked per Week
description: Usual hours worked per week in main job. Zero when not employed.
notes: Zero when employed=0.

label: Self-Reported Health Status
description: Respondent's self-assessed general health
notes: Ordered categorical. Recode to numeric for ordered probit/logit.

label: Marital Status
description: Whether the respondent is currently married or in a registered partnership
notes: Standard control in earnings regressions.

label: Number of Children
description: Number of own children in the household under age 18
notes: Used to analyse family gap in wages for women.

label: Urban Residence
description: Whether the respondent lives in an urban or metropolitan area
notes: Associated with higher wages due to agglomeration effects.
